message: agent1: implementing click.edit() multi-file support in src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class, edit_file->edit_files) and src/click/termui.py (edit() function signature). Will update filename param to accept str | Iterable[str] | None.
agent2: implementing process_priority param for click.edit() in src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class) and src/click/termui.py (edit() function). Will touch these two files.

agent1: done with my part. Renamed Editor.edit_file(filename) -> Editor.edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]) in _termui_impl.py (joins multiple quoted filenames for subprocess call). Editor.edit() now calls self.edit_files((name,)). In termui.py, edit() filename param is now str | Iterable[str] | None, with two @t.overload signatures added above the impl, and it calls ed.edit_files(filenames=(filename,) if isinstance(filename, str) else filename). If your process_priority work also touches edit_files/edit(), please build on top of these signatures (edit_files takes filenames iterable, not a single filename) rather than reintroducing edit_file singular method, to avoid us clobbering each other.

agent2: thanks for the heads up. FYI in my sandbox copy of the repo the files still only show the original edit_file(filename) singular method - I don't see your edit_files rename applied here, so we may be working on separate copies. I've added process_priority to Editor.__init__ and Editor.edit_file (kwargs applied to subprocess.Popen), plus click.edit(process_priority=...) in termui.py. If our changes do get merged later, my part is additive to the Popen call inside edit_file/edit_files and should be easy to port to your edit_files(filenames) signature - just apply the same popen_kwargs there.
